title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Evan Bates Praises Figure Skating's Next Generation

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Evan Bates praises the next generation of figure skaters and says the future of the sport has never looked brighter. The short Team USA video conveys that optimistic message and points viewers to Team USA's digital channels.
bullets: Evan Bates highlights optimism about figure skating's next generation.
Description states the future of the sport "has never looked brighter."
Video published by Team USA on YouTube (2026-02-05).
No transcript is available for the video.
Description includes links to Team USA's website and social platforms.
takeaways: Promotional athlete content around Olympic cycles can drive spikes in streaming and CDN demand — ensure scalable delivery capacity.
Cross-platform distribution (YouTube plus social links) requires reliable bandwidth and redundancy to maintain uptime during engagement surges.
Low-latency delivery and caching strategies improve viewer experience for short-form, high-engagement sports content.
Plan for storage and quick retrieval of promotional media assets to support rapid re-use across channels during event windows.

items.title: re:Invent - Chalk Talk - Solving private IPv4 exhaustion using AWS Cloud WAN & Amazon VPC Lattice

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this session addresses private IPv4 exhaustion caused by limited address planning, architectural shifts, mergers, and IP‑hungry technologies like containers. It presents a scalable, cost‑effective approach that combines AWS Cloud WAN (with service insertion) and Amazon VPC Lattice to centralize private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink, maximizing IP utilization and simplifying network management across distributed environments.
bullets: Causes of private IPv4 exhaustion: limited planning, architecture changes, mergers, and containerized workloads
Solution combines AWS Cloud WAN (service insertion) with Amazon VPC Lattice
Centralizes private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink for shared services
Benefits highlighted: maximize IP space utilization, reduce costs, and simplify network management
Designed to support evolving infrastructure needs across distributed environments
takeaways: IPv4 exhaustion can block cloud scalability and new project launches—addressing it is operationally important
Centralizing NAT and PrivateLink via Cloud WAN + VPC Lattice can reclaim and better utilize scarce IP space
The combined architecture aims to reduce network costs and operational overhead for distributed environments
This approach offers a durable pattern to accommodate mergers, architectural shifts, and IP‑intensive workloads

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York for $27K with plans to build Airbnb properties. Over the next three years he commuted on weekends from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself, added more cabins, and by September 2025 the property had generated over $2.1 million with luxury cabins renting around $700/night.
bullets: 2015: Chris Broomfield purchased five acres in upstate New York for $27K to develop Airbnb rentals.
He built an A-frame cabin alone over three years, commuting on weekends from Connecticut.
Additional cabins were added after the initial build.
Cabins are marketed as luxury rentals at about $700 per night.
As of September 2025 the property has brought in over $2.1 million in revenue.
takeaways: Demonstrates that low-cost rural land can be developed into high-revenue short-term rental assets, implying potential new demand for local utility capacity and connectivity.
Incremental, owner-led development can produce steady, compounding economic returns—relevant when forecasting gradual load growth in rural areas.
High nightly rates and successful occupancy suggest seasonal or peak usage patterns that may affect local infrastructure planning and resilience.
Distributed hospitality investments outside urban centers could increase distributed energy and network needs over time.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Eksperci KPMG omawiają, jak patrzeć na zmiany operacyjne (np. przeniesienie produkcji, dystrybucji, transfer pracowników) z perspektywy cen transferowych, wycen oraz obowiązków raportowych. Podkreślają znaczenie terminowej dokumentacji restrukturyzacji, opisują przykłady pułapek przy przenoszeniu kontraktów i proponują checklistę działań dla firm.
bullets: Definicja restrukturyzacji w kontekście cen transferowych i kryteria istotności zmian (analiza EBIT).
Znaczenie dokumentacji restrukturyzacji: przygotowanie „na czas” minimalizuje ryzyko w przyszłości.
Obowiązki raportowe związane z restrukturyzacją, m.in. TPR i MDR.
Rola wycen w procesie restrukturyzacji i najczęstsze błędy praktyczne.
Typowe scenariusze: przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów i związane pułapki oceny.
takeaways: Przy planowaniu przeniesienia mocy produkcyjnych lub usług sprawdź wpływ na ceny transferowe i konieczność raportowania (TPR/MDR).
Dokumentacja restrukturyzacji powinna być przygotowana równolegle z działaniami operacyjnymi, by ograniczyć ryzyko kontroli i sporów.
Wyceny należy zlecić wcześnie — brak rzetelnej wyceny to częsty błąd przy zmianie profilu działalności lub transferze pracowników.
Analiza wpływu na EBIT pomaga ustalić materialność zmiany i zakres wymaganej dokumentacji.
Włącz checklistę działań związanych z TP/wycenami do planów projektowych przy przenoszeniu kontraktów lub zespołów.

items.title: AWS Power Hour: Generative AI For Developers | S1 E1 | Exam Overview and FM Integrations

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this episode breaks down the GenAI-focused exam format and study strategies while guiding developers through designing resilient generative AI architectures. It explains how to build data processing pipelines for foundation models, implement vector stores for RAG systems, apply prompt engineering techniques, and analyzes sample exam questions alongside real-world solutions for advanced GenAI systems.
bullets: Overview of the exam format and recommended study strategies for developers.
Guidance on designing resilient generative AI architectures.
How to build data processing pipelines tailored to foundation models.
Implementing vector stores to support retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems.
Prompt engineering methods and analysis of sample exam questions with real-world solutions.
takeaways: Plan capacity and scalability for foundation-model workloads and their data pipelines, as the episode emphasizes pipeline design for these systems.
Provide low-latency, reliable storage and compute to support vector stores used in RAG architectures.
Prioritize availability and redundancy when provisioning infrastructure to meet the resilience needs of advanced GenAI architectures.
Support staff training and certification efforts—exam format and study strategies covered can guide workforce readiness for deploying these systems.

takeaways: The content is a CTA to trial Copilot—consider using the trial to evaluate if it meets your operational needs.
Assess whether Copilot's "backup" positioning aligns with your decision-making or workflow support requirements.
Follow the link in the bio to start a hands-on evaluation before any internal adoption decisions.
Use trial results to inform stakeholder discussions about fit and next steps.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Marcus Oliver of Google Delta says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy, and that this cost is not primarily about computing resources. He recommends reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to deliver real business outcomes.
bullets: Most expensive AI mistake: rushing to code without a clear strategy (not compute costs).
Focus on reimagining value chains before building models or systems.
Deploy multi-agent systems as a way to achieve tangible business outcomes.
Advice comes from Marcus Oliver, lead of Google Delta (Google Cloud professional services).
takeaways: Prioritize a clear AI strategy and value-chain mapping before investing in compute or rapid prototyping.
Align infrastructure and capacity planning to business outcomes rather than raw GPU/compute forecasts.
Design architectures that support multi-agent systems if those systems map to your operational value chain.
Consider engaging professional services or expert guidance to avoid the coding trap and accelerate measurable ROI.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility, policy controls, red teaming, and a real-time AI firewall. It is designed to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats.
bullets: AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across user interactions with AI systems
AI Red Teaming: stress-tests applications and models against real-world attacks
AI Firewall: real-time guardrails securing prompts and responses
Unified platform to protect AI agents, applications, and user interactions
Aims to eliminate shadow AI and safeguard infrastructure from evolving threats
takeaways: Visibility and policy controls are essential to detect and eliminate unauthorized AI use (shadow AI).
Regular AI red teaming should be integrated into security assessments to uncover real-world vulnerabilities.
Real-time prompt/response guardrails can reduce the risk of data exposure and malicious outputs.
A unified AI security platform on Google Cloud can simplify management of agent and application protection.

Short promotional clips are used to raise awareness—plan communications and training to convert interest into practical use.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… NVIDIA GeForce highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community build, created by u/JeffersonPutnam, featuring a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The video description lists full system specs including a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB DDR5-6000 RAM, Corsair SF1000 power supply, and a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build from the Summer of RTX contest.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small-form-factor).
GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
Core components: MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB Teamgroup DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
GeForce host: Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato) and PCPartPicker parts list linked.
takeaways: Latest consumer GPU generation: The build uses a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, indicating availability of current-generation consumer GPUs for high-performance desktop projects.
Power provisioning matters: The system pairs the RTX 5090 with a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000), underscoring significant power requirements to accommodate top-tier GPUs in compact builds.

Cooling and form-factor constraints: The use of a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) in an SFF chassis highlights the need for effective liquid cooling solutions when integrating high-end GPUs into tight enclosures.
Motherboard and layout: An ITX-class board (MSI MPG B850I Edge) was chosen, showing component selection and spatial/layout considerations required to support high-performance parts in small systems.
